name: TDD-STATE-MACHINE-BACKLOG-1
title: Improve TDD step machine: solid state names, spec skill extraction, and bypassable human review gate
description:
Three related improvements to the TDD state machine:
1. SOLID STATE NAMES + CLEAR ACTIONS: Current state names are ambiguous. Each state should have a canonical name and a precisely defined set of allowed/required actions. No implicit transitions.
2. SPEC SKILL EXTRACTION: The spec-authoring task is currently embedded inside the TDD cycle. It should be peeled off into a standalone skill so it can be invoked independently (e.g. during backlog grooming or design phases) without triggering a full TDD cycle.
3. HUMAN REVIEW GATE FOR SPEC + PLAN: After spec is authored and before implementation begins, the agent should pause and present the spec change and planned implementation steps to the user for review. This gate must be bypassable (one-time or session-long flag) for cases where the user wants fully autonomous execution.

history.note: Deferred: too large/complex for housekeeping waves. Requires dedicated design cycle. Filed for future roadmap consideration.
substantive pre-existing item (filed 2026-05-15); reopening for planning/implementation per user audit 2026-05-18
Temporarily closed before CONTRACT-BACKLOG-19 housekeeping wave to prevent accidental closure by wave b/b filings; will be reopened after the wave completes

Wave 6 audit close — superseded. Item-1 (solid state names + canonical actions): delivered via Inv 1 (valid state set), Inv 2 (primary forward order), Inv 3 (alternate forward edge), Inv 4 (backward requires --force), Inv 5 (deprecated terminal) — five sequential confined invariants pin the entire state semantics. Item-2 (spec skill extraction): delivered via the rabbit-feature-spec skill (.claude/skills/rabbit-feature-spec/), which is invoked standalone for spec authoring and is also called from rabbit-feature-touch Step 3. Item-3 (human-review gate, bypassable): delivered via rabbit-feature-touch Step 4 which surfaces the impl-suggestion summary by default and is bypassable via the .rabbit-human-approval-bypass marker (managed by /rabbit-config human-approval true|false). All three items shipped post-filing.
